description: Curated gut microbiome knowledge base for RAG explanations

Faecalibacterium.mechanism: Produces butyrate that fuels colonocytes and supports epithelial barrier integrity.
Faecalibacterium.phenotype: Often depleted in IBD and metabolic inflammation.

Faecalibacterium prausnitzii.mechanism: Anti-inflammatory commensal; butyrate and microbial anti-inflammatory molecule (MAM).
Faecalibacterium prausnitzii.phenotype: Reduced abundance associated with Crohn's disease activity.

Akkermansia muciniphila.mechanism: Mucin-degrading specialist that can reinforce mucus layer dynamics and metabolic signaling.
Akkermansia muciniphila.phenotype: Associated with metabolic health; context-dependent in inflammation.

Escherichia.mechanism: Includes adherent-invasive lineages that can exploit inflamed mucosa.

Bacteroides.mechanism: Polysaccharide utilization and immunomodulatory metabolite production.
Bacteroides.phenotype: Core genus of healthy adult gut microbiota.

Bifidobacterium.mechanism: SCFA production and early-life colonization benefits.

Prevotella.mechanism: Fiber-associated community member influencing mucosal immune tone.

Roseburia.mechanism: Butyrate-producing Firmicute supporting gut barrier function.
Roseburia.phenotype: Frequently reduced in inflammatory gut conditions.

Blautia.mechanism: Acetogen involved in cross-feeding and bile acid metabolism.
Blautia.phenotype: Altered in metabolic syndrome and IBD cohorts.

Ruminococcus.mechanism: Complex carbohydrate degradation including resistant starch.
